meta_title: For Students: client workflow to Solve no design skills (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to client workflow and overcome no design skills. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Client Workflow to Solve No Design Skills
content.seo_description: Master professional client workflows to bypass design hurdles. An Intermediate Checklist for students using Antigravity and Claude Code to build SaaS apps.
content.answer_block: Eliminate design anxiety by treating UI as a functional constraint rather than an aesthetic hurdle. Use Antigravity to scaffold structured layouts and Claude Code to enforce consistent design tokens. By adopting a system-first client workflow, you transform your lack of design intuition into a repeatable, modular engineering process.
content.intro:
You stare at the blank editor, your logic is airtight, but the UI looks like a relic from 1998. The 'no-design' barrier is the silent killer of student projects, turning high-potential SaaS builds into abandoned repositories. You don't need a degree in graphic design; you need a rigorous, engineering-led workflow that treats visuals as data.

Today, we pivot. We are moving away from 'making it look pretty' and toward 'designing for systems.' By leveraging Antigravity for structural scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative refinement, you will bridge the gap between functional code and professional-grade application interfaces.

steps.title: Define Design Tokens as Logic
steps.description: Stop guessing colors and spacing. Establish a JSON-based design system that dictates every visual choice, ensuring consistency without artistic intuition.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a design-tokens.json file containing a primary color palette, spacing scale (4px increments), and typography hierarchy based on professional SaaS standards.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'apply design-tokens.json to all CSS modules'

steps.title: Scaffold Structural Skeleton
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate high-fidelity wireframe components that focus exclusively on layout flow rather than decorative elements.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a modular React layout skeleton using Tailwind CSS that implements a sidebar-nav and main-content area, adhering to the previously defined spacing scale.

steps.title: Implement Semantic UI Patterns
steps.description: Automate the creation of repetitive components like inputs and buttons to ensure your app feels cohesive at every interaction point.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a library of base UI components (Button, Input, Card) that strictly consume our design tokens for colors, shadows, and border-radius.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'refactor existing components to use the new UI library'

steps.title: Automate Responsive Breakpoints
steps.description: Force your application to handle multiple screen sizes by programmatically defining responsive grids rather than manual tweaking.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a responsive grid system configuration that scales from mobile to desktop using Tailwind utility classes.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'verify responsiveness across all pages'

steps.title: Final Visual Polish via AI Review
steps.description: Use Claude Code to perform a final audit of your interface against the design tokens to identify any 'off-brand' styling inconsistencies.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a design engineer. Review the codebase for any hardcoded hex values or non-standard spacing, and suggest replacements using our token system.

pro_tips: Treat your CSS as configuration data, not art. If you can't describe the style in a variable, it shouldn't exist in your codebase.
Use 'Dark Mode' as a primary development constraint early on; it forces better contrast management and cleaner color token usage.
Limit your palette to 3 primary colors. Complexity is the enemy of the non-designer; constraints are your best friend.
Leverage Claude Code to document your design system as you build; this turns your project into a professional-grade portfolio piece.
Prioritize whitespace. If an element feels 'wrong,' the solution is almost always adding 16px of padding, not changing the color.
content.next_steps: Once your UI foundation is solid, deploy your project to Vercel and document the 'Engineering-First' approach in your README to impress recruiters.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting design anxiety stall your engineering progress. Master the workflow that turns code into high-converting products. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
